title: A brain rhythm from two populations
author: OpenDiscover
blurb: No single neuron here — just two whole populations, one excitatory and one inhibitory, chasing each other. Excitation ramps activity up; inhibition (lagging behind) reins it back in; the overshoot repeats, and the network locks into a self-sustaining rhythm. This is the Wilson–Cowan (1972) mean-field origin of cortical oscillations like the gamma rhythm. The limit cycle exists only inside a band of drive: too little and the tissue is quiet, too much and it saturates. Here P=2.2 sits in the sweet spot.
